channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Pakistan urges restraint as its Mideast deal unravels
item.description: Pakistan urges restraint as Iran and the U.S. clash and a memorandum of understanding unravels.
item.content:encoded: Pakistan urges restraint as Iran and the U.S. clash and a memorandum of understanding unravels.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: People watching the World Cup during work hours is leading to a drop in productivity
item.description: The global economy may be suffering from lost productivity as people get swept by World Cup fever, a survey shows. This small business owner would know. She's spent most of her days watching matches.
item.content:encoded: The global economy may be suffering from lost productivity as people get swept by World Cup fever, a survey shows. This small business owner would know. She's spent most of her days watching matches.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: The U.S. strikes Iran after Trump announces a renewed blockade and tolls in Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. and Iran are fighting for control of the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a return to all-out war after agreeing to a ceasefire last month.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Iran are fighting for control of the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a return to all-out war after agreeing to a ceasefire last month.

(Image credit: Razieh Poudat)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire grows more distant. And, Congress faces a consequential week
item.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire over the weekend amid tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. And, Congress returns from recess facing tight deadlines.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire over the weekend amid tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. And, Congress returns from recess facing tight deadlines.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Sam Neill, known for 'Jurassic Park' and 'The Piano,' dies at 78, his family says
item.description: In 2023, Neill disclosed he had been diagnosed with angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma, a rare type of non-Hodgkin lymphoma. Neill died on Monday in Sydney, according to a statement posted to the actor's social media page.
item.content:encoded:
In 2023, Neill disclosed he had been diagnosed with angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma, a rare type of non-Hodgkin lymphoma. Neill died on Monday in Sydney, according to a statement posted to the actor's social media page.

(Image credit: Richard Shotwell/AP Photo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What would it mean for Ukraine if it actually starts producing Patriot missiles?
item.description: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Seth Jones from the Center for Strategic and International Studies about President Trump granting Ukraine permission to produce Patriot missiles.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Seth Jones from the Center for Strategic and International Studies about President Trump granting Ukraine permission to produce Patriot missiles.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Investigators probe a fire at a music bar in Bangkok that killed at least 27 people
item.description: By Monday morning, forensic teams were investigating the scene. Officials say many victims were trapped in windowless bathrooms near a rear exit. Investigators are focusing on the ceiling above the performance stage for clues.
item.content:encoded:
By Monday morning, forensic teams were investigating the scene. Officials say many victims were trapped in windowless bathrooms near a rear exit. Investigators are focusing on the ceiling above the performance stage for clues.

(Image credit: Bangkok Metropolitan Administrat)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Toronto police looking for suspects after deadly shooting at festival
item.description: At least two people are dead and at least four people are injured after a shooting on Saturday night at a festival celebrating Latin culture in Canada's biggest city.
item.content:encoded:
At least two people are dead and at least four people are injured after a shooting on Saturday night at a festival celebrating Latin culture in Canada's biggest city.

(Image credit: Keito Newman)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Alvarez's 112th-minute goal helps lift Argentina past Switzerland 3-1 and into World Cup semifinals
item.description: Julian Alvarez sent defending champion Argentina into the World Cup semifinals with a long-range strike in the 112th minute against Switzerland.
item.content:encoded:
Julian Alvarez sent defending champion Argentina into the World Cup semifinals with a long-range strike in the 112th minute against Switzerland.

(Image credit: Abbie Parr)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. launches new strikes on Iran and Tehran attacks U.S. allies in the Gulf
item.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire for the third weekend in a row, with strikes stretching into Monday as Iran responded to U.S. attacks by targeting U.S. allies in the region.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire for the third weekend in a row, with strikes stretching into Monday as Iran responded to U.S. attacks by targeting U.S. allies in the region.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff
